topic: High-Level Synthesis for Scheduling Pipelined Circuits

entries.objective: Review the technical correctness of the initiation-interval analysis.
entries.prompt: Check the ResII, RecII, latency, throughput, and scheduling logic in my HLS seminar paper.
entries.ai_output_summary: The AI identified that the original accumulation example mixed an intra-iteration dependency with a loop-carried recurrence and that operation latency and resource initiation capability were not modeled consistently.

actions: Compared the corrected definitions with the cited modulo-scheduling literature.
Recalculated ResII and RecII for all three examples.
Checked every schedule against the corrected dependency inequality.

issues: The original accumulation example attributed II=2 to the wrong dependency.
The original tables mixed operation duration and operation start times.

entries.reflection: The interaction helped me correct the main technical weakness of the paper and separate latency, resource initiation interval, and recurrence constraints.

entries.objective: Improve the explanation of modulo scheduling in student-level language.
entries.prompt: Explain modulo scheduling, ResII, RecII, and IImin in simple academic language that I can understand and present.
entries.ai_output_summary: The AI provided simplified explanations of modulo slots, resource bounds, loop-carried cycles, and throughput.

actions: Checked the formulas against the cited primary and textbook sources.
Kept only explanations that matched the mathematical definitions.

issues: Some early explanations were too simplified and were made more precise.

entries.reflection: This helped me explain the concepts clearly without hiding the mathematical meaning.

entries.objective: Improve grammar and scientific readability of my paper.
entries.prompt: Correct the grammar and improve the clarity of my HLS seminar paper while keeping a simple student-level tone.
entries.ai_output_summary: The AI suggested shorter sentences, clearer transitions, and removal of repeated or overly confident claims.

actions: Rejected wording that changed my intended technical meaning.
Checked revised claims against the calculations and cited sources.

issues: Some wording was too formal and was simplified.

entries.reflection: The AI was used as a grammar and clarity reviewer. I kept control of the final wording.

entries.objective: Improve the structure of the paper and connect it to hardware/software co-design.
entries.prompt: Suggest a clearer IEEE-style structure and show how the software scheduler connects to hardware resource decisions.
entries.ai_output_summary: The AI suggested a clearer problem-background-algorithm-experiment-discussion flow and a small hardware/software co-design section.

actions: Compared the structure with the seminar requirements.
Checked that the co-design section matched the implemented workflow.
Removed sections that were not supported by the work.

issues: The first structure was too detailed for the available page length.

entries.reflection: The interaction helped connect the C++ scheduling tool with the resulting hardware resource choices.

entries.objective: Improve LaTeX figures, tables, listings, and final-page layout.
entries.prompt: Fix IEEE LaTeX formatting, balance the final page, and improve the readability of diagrams, code listings, and tables.
entries.ai_output_summary: The AI suggested compact TikZ diagrams, shorter tables, controlled float spacing, and final-column balancing.

actions: Compiled the document repeatedly with pdflatex.
Rendered all pages to images and checked for clipping, overlap, and unreadable text.
Adjusted the final layout after visual inspection.

issues: Some early layouts produced poor page balance or too much empty space.

entries.reflection: The formatting suggestions were accepted only after successful compilation and visual checking.
